## Equations
### single_mode_partition_z_R — Single-mode partition function / Hilbert series z_R(x) = sum_n d_n x^n (Eq. single_mode_Z)

*definition_and_observable_source*

$$
For a single mode with H = eps*n, z_R(e^{-beta eps}) = Tr[e^{-beta eps n}] = sum_{n>=0} d_n e^{-n beta eps}, with x=e^{-beta eps}. d_n is the dimension of the n-particle single-mode space (generalized exclusion statistics). Table 1 R-matrix examples: Ex.1 (decoupled, -delta_ad delta_bc) and Ex.2 (Green, delta_ad delta_bc (-1)^{delta_ab}) both give z=(1+x)^m; Ex.3 (-delta_ac delta_bd) gives z=1+m x; Ex.4 (lambda_ab xi_cd - delta_ac delta_bd, with lambda xi lambda^T xi^T = 1, Tr(lambda xi^T)=2) gives z=1+m x+x^2. Ordinary limits: fermion z=1+x, boson z=1/(1-x).
$$

status: `verified` · source: main text Eq. single_mode_Z; Table 1 (tab:Hilbert_series); SI Sec. 'Calculation of exclusion statistics and single mode partition functions'

Code: `src/paraparticles.py::Species.degeneracies`, `src/paraparticles.py::species_table`


### thermal_occupation_n_beta — Free-paraparticle thermal occupation <n_k>_beta = x z'_R(x)/z_R(x) (Eq. n_k_expectation)

*main_observable*

$$
For the free (bilinear) Hamiltonian H = sum_{ij} h_{ij} e_{ij} = sum_k eps_k n_k with eps_k = eig(h), the whole partition function factorizes into single-mode ones, giving <n_k>_beta = z'_R(e^{-beta eps_k}) e^{-beta eps_k} / z_R(e^{-beta eps_k}) = x z'_R(x)/z_R(x) with x=e^{-beta eps_k}. Equivalently <n> = sum_n n d_n x^n / sum_n d_n x^n. Fermion: x/(1+x)=1/(e^{beta eps}+1). Boson: x/(1-x)=1/(e^{beta eps}-1).
$$

status: `verified` · source: main text Eq. n_k_expectation; main text 'Exact solution of free paraparticles'

Code: `src/paraparticles.py::Species.occupation`, `scripts/gen_fig2.py`


### solvable_1d_spin_model_free_paraparticles — 1D solvable spin model maps to free paraparticles (Eq. Hamil1Dspin)

*physical_realization_validation*

$$
For the Ex.3 R-matrix, the (m+1)-level spin chain H = sum_{i,a} J_i (x^+_{i,a} y^-_{i+1,a} + x^-_{i,a} y^+_{i+1,a}) - sum_{i,a} mu_i y^+_{i,a} y^-_{i,a} with x^pm=y^pm, y^+_a|0>=|1,a>, is mapped by a generalized Jordan-Wigner transformation onto free paraparticles H=sum_{ij} h_{ij} e_{ij}, h tridiagonal with h_{i,i+1}=h_{i+1,i}=J_i, h_{ii}=-mu_i. Consequently the many-body spectrum equals sum_{k in S} eps_k (eps_k=eig(h)) with multiplicity m^{|S|}, S subseteq modes.
$$

status: `verified` · source: main text Eq. Hamil1Dspin and 'Emergent paraparticles in condensed matter systems'; SI 'Details on the 1D spin model and the MPO JWT'

Code: `src/spin_model_1d.py`, `scripts/run_ed_validation.py`
